title: Which Japan Region Matches Your Interests?

results.title: Kyoto and Kansai Region
results.description: You are drawn to the heart of traditional Japan. Kyoto's thousands of temples, geisha districts, and preserved Edo-period townscapes align perfectly with your passion for historical depth and cultural authenticity. The broader Kansai region offers access to Nara's ancient Buddhist heritage and Osaka's vibrant local food scene.

results.title: Hiroshima, Okayama, and Western Honshu
results.description: Your interests reflect a balance between history and natural beauty. This region offers profound historical sites alongside stunning mountain temples, traditional garden arts, and the scenic Seto Inland Sea. You'll find deep cultural experiences without the overwhelming crowds of central tourist zones.

results.title: Takayama and the Japanese Alps
results.description: You're drawn to where tradition meets pristine nature. The mountain villages of the Takayama region preserve Edo-period merchant houses and offer authentic rural culture alongside spectacular seasonal landscapes. This is ideal for travelers seeking solitude and deep cultural immersion away from urban centers.

results.title: Tokyo and Yokohama Metropolis
results.description: Your interests lean toward contemporary Japanese culture and modern heritage. Tokyo's museums, innovative neighborhoods, and blend of ultra-modern and traditional elements suit explorers who value cultural dynamism and cutting-edge creative scenes alongside selective historical sites.

options.text: Edo Period samurai culture and feudal traditions

description: Discover which region of Japan aligns best with your passions for history, culture, and heritage. Based on your interests and travel priorities, we'll match you to the Japanese region that feels like home.
